ip_outcome_note: The card art depicts a massive lavender-furred yeti viewed from behind, set against a pale sky-blue background. The FFTCG card explicitly labels this figure as category FFVI with 'Yeti' job class. The vision description — large lavender/pink fur, massive round-shouldered build, pale sky background — matches Umaro's canonical sprite and artwork across all Final Fantasy VI media. Additionally, a retailer (Total Cards) specifically lists this card as 'Final Fantasy Opus 8 - Umaro - (8-024C)' and the Patreon community roundup references Umaro's card text by name. IP verified as internal Square Enix / Final Fantasy VI property.

trivia_bullets.text: Umaro is one of two optional secret party members in Final Fantasy VI (the other being Gogo). He is a yeti found in the Yeti's Cave in the Narshe Cliffs during the World of Ruin — the game's second half after Kefka destroys the world. Mog must be in the party to recruit him; once defeated in battle, Umaro submits to Mog as his 'boss.' [Final Fantasy Wiki: Umaro (Final Fantasy VI party member)]
sources: Final Fantasy Wiki (Fandom): Umaro (Final Fantasy VI party member)
Gameranx: Final Fantasy VI Pixel Remaster — How to Recruit Umaro

trivia_bullets.text: In Final Fantasy VI, Umaro is a Berserker — he cannot be directly controlled in battle. His actions are determined by equipped relics: the Berserker Ring causes him to throw party members at enemies, the Rage Ring enables glacial wind attacks, and without relics he uses a powerful physical attack. He cannot equip Magicite and therefore cannot learn magic — making him one of the most purely physical characters in the game. [Final Fantasy Wiki: Umaro (Final Fantasy VI party member)]
sources: Final Fantasy Wiki (Fandom): Umaro (Final Fantasy VI party member)

trivia_bullets.text: Umaro's FFTCG card (8-024C) is a Common-rarity Ice-element Forward. His card text includes: 'When Umaro enters the field, you may search for 1 monster and add it to your hand. Umaro must attack once per turn if possible.' His special ability 'Bodyslam' deals 7,000 damage to a target forward — a direct translation of his in-game physical attack that reflects the Berserker-must-attack mechanic. [Community research via WebSearch: totalcards.net Umaro listing]

Umaro's lore highlights an unusual detail: despite being a large yeti with limited speech (mostly grunts and groans), he is capable of crafting crude weapons and armor, and his cave features a bone carving and what appears to be a makeshift carpet. He only follows the party because Mog asserts himself as Umaro's 'boss' — Umaro demonstrates deep loyalty to Mog specifically, and by extension the broader party. [Final Fantasy Wiki: Umaro (Final Fantasy VI party member)]
sources: Final Fantasy Wiki (Fandom): Umaro (Final Fantasy VI party member)

related_cards.name: Mog
